### 1.3a The lifecycle ledger — KOT-LIFE/1

The comparison this programme runs is a *deployment-efficiency* comparison, not an
equal-total-investment comparison — legitimate, but it must be named accurately
and priced [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, answering review "add a lifecycle ledger"].

- DECISION: every W1-relevant system (S and every comparator) publishes a
  **lifecycle ledger**: (1) donor pretraining provenance and compute where known
  (dimension-drop arms *inherit* their donor's training compute — stated, never
  netted out); (2) architecture fine-tuning + hyperparameter-search compute
  actually spent (the tuning-symmetry audit trail, §2.3); (3) store costs:
  authoring, parsing, embedding, indexing, and human review, in hours and $;
  (4) total cost of ownership amortised at three pinned deployment volumes —
  10^3, 10^6, 10^9 queries — using the measured KOT-COST/2 energy/latency figures.
  Training longer to obtain a smaller model is a real deployment strategy and
  optimal size depends on inference volume (REVIEW-CITED: Sardana et al., "Beyond
  Chinchilla-Optimal"; verified by P3-LR-SYS). The ledger CO-REPORTS with every W1
  claim and feeds kill condition K-P3v2(6); it does not gate W1 numerically
  because store-authoring hours and GPU-hours have no non-arbitrary common unit —
  the honesty mechanism is mandatory publication + the kill condition, not a

- Rationale: the only family with a measured system-level cost win shape
  (+0.1507 at ~10% cost — formal inputs, alignment-confounded; §0).
- Evidence status: supported at formal-input scope; cautioned by the NL-boundary
  FAILs (the loop's real-input version does not exist until the NLB gate clears)
  and by the unresolved attribution (knull / human f2b-transfer unrun).
- **G1 coverage upper-bound kill (run FIRST, no GPU):** compute
  `Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b · κ_b · (1 − a_b^cov)` where w_b is benchmark b's index weight,
  κ_b the store-covered fraction of its items, and a_b^cov the baseline's accuracy
  on covered items — the index gain if EVERY covered item were corrected
  perfectly. If Δ_max < δ_k, the index-mover claim is killed on paper
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0817; the review's formula adopted verbatim]. Given the
  measured external coverage (§0: 0/1,550 define-lane, g8 0/1,000, m0b 0.3542
  friendliest-corpus), this gate is expected to bind hard and early — that is its
  job.
- G4 comparator set (expanded per the review): compute-optimal/adaptive neural
  sampling (not naive best-of-k); a learned neural verifier at matched budget;
  generic structured RAG over the same corpus; grammar/constrained decoding
  without the engine; the same loop with the aligned non-kernel store and with
  the matched generic store.
- Cheapest falsifier: G1 above (~$0); then, if it survives, the R-1 index test on
  natural benchmark text with the NLB-gated front-end inside the measured system.

### 3.2 H-GNN-{ST, KV, LF} — hybrid GNN over the kernel+world graph

The kernel + world layer form a typed relational graph (concept URNs, axiom
records, world-layer edges). A small GNN encodes query-relevant subgraphs; the
encoding is fused into the LM at one of three seams:

| Variant | Fusion seam | Continuity | Expected cost/size delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| H-GNN-ST | GNN output → trained bridge → soft tokens at the input edge | The A2/E5 adapter seam, replicated positive at one rung | +GNN weights (MBs) + adapter; prefill shortens if soft tokens replace text serialisation |
| H-GNN-KV | Graph nodes written as KV pairs into attention (KBLaM-style) | L2b kernel-addressed memory (ladder) | +KV construction cost per query; memory grows with injected node count |
| H-GNN-LF | Late fusion at logits/answer-selection | none (new) | cheapest to build, weakest hypothesised integration |

- Evidence status: unmeasured as a family; the *seam* is supported (E5 adapter
  PASS at one rung); cautioned hard by nsk1 — text-delivered grounding measured
  net-harmful and internal delivery reached only echo grade with integration
  unresolved (§0), so "the GNN delivers but the LM doesn't use it" is the named,
  measured-adjacent failure mode.
- **Falsifier, rescoped (accepting the review's correction):** "fusion ≤
  text-of-graph on tiny graphs at R-0/R-1" does NOT kill the family — text is
  often superior for tiny graphs; graph encoders may only pay off as graph size
  and reasoning depth grow. The kill is now: fusion ≤ text-of-graph at matched
  information and resource budgets ACROSS a pre-registered sweep of subgraph
  size × reasoning depth (including the D4 held-out relation compositions and
  proof depths) at R-1 — a flat or text-favouring curve over the whole sweep kills
  the family at scope; a depth-increasing fusion advantage promotes it even if
  tiny graphs favour text. Registered projection: ASM-0805.
- Controls (per the review, all pre-registered): same retrieved nodes with no
  edges; shuffled edges and relation labels; MLP/DeepSets/plain-Transformer
  encoders over the same inputs; text serialisation at equal information and
  resource budgets; held-out relation compositions and proof depths; **oracle
  subgraph vs NL-extracted subgraph** — the NL-extracted leg is NLB-gated per
  ASM-0814, the oracle leg is `oracle-diagnostic`.
- **Four-stage decomposition, separately instrumented:** retrieval → graph
  construction → graph encoding → LM use. A positive delivery probe is
  insufficient; the endpoint is causal behavioural improvement on held-out items
  (the nsk1 lesson, enforced by design).

### 3.3 H-RULE-{CD, KV, AD, ACT, HL} — rules-based inference built into the LLM: the placement analysis

The maintainer asked *where* deterministic rule-inference should live inside the
model. Five placements, analysed on the axes that matter. Two corrections from
the review are folded into the table and rulings: (a) **constrained decoding is
transport, not inference** — CD enforces an output language; the *engine* must
have already derived the valid continuation set, so H-RULE-CD is precisely
"engine-derived continuation sets delivered via masking", and without the
executor it is just a grammar; (b) **attention weights are not causal
provenance** — KV/HL provenance claims require causal ablation, intervention, or
executor traces [STIPULATED: ASM-0815; corrects technical errors #6 and #8].

| Placement | Mechanism | Inference cost | Invasiveness | Provenance | Fail-closed | Prior evidence hook |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **H-RULE-CD** constrained-decoding head | engine derives valid continuation sets for covered spans mid-decode; masking delivers them; uncovered decode untouched | engine µs + masking, near-zero overhead | none (no weights touched) | exact (record id per constrained span; the engine's derivation IS the provenance) | yes by construction | L3c (ladder); f2b loop is the unconstrained cousin |
| **H-RULE-KV** KV-side rule memory | rule heads/records inserted as KV pairs keyed by kernel content-hash; attention reads them | KV build per query; grows with fired-rule count | low (no base-weight change) | requires causal ablation/intervention or executor traces — attention maps do NOT qualify | partial | L2b (ladder); KBLaM-family lit |
| **H-RULE-AD** rule adapters | LoRA-class adapters trained to implement rule families; swapped/composed at inference | adapter matmuls, small | medium (trained, but detachable) | none intrinsically | no — must be measured | A2/E5 trained-bridge precedent (input-side only) |
| **H-RULE-ACT** activation-level rule firing | rule conditions detected in activations; firing applies steering vectors / activation edits | probe + edit per layer, small | medium-high (runtime intervention) | weak; causal validation required | no — edits can corrupt off-target behaviour | nsk1 residual channel: delivery ECHO-grade, integration unresolved — the direct caution |
| **H-RULE-HL** dedicated hidden layer | a mid-network layer whose units are pinned to kernel concepts; rule inference as a deterministic map on that bottleneck | in the forward pass (cheap) but always-on | highest (architecture surgery, training from scratch or heavy retrofit) | naming units is NOT interpretability — bottleneck provenance must be causally validated (ablate/patch) | no — bottleneck can throttle general capability | L2a (ladder); the φ-sweep L2c is its dose-response |

Design ruling from the analysis (a prior ordering, not a verdict):

- DECISION: Phase-2 tests the placements in the order CD → KV → {AD, ACT} → HL —
  cheapest, least invasive, most provenance-preserving, most fail-closed first;
  HL is deferred until a shallower placement has shown a positive, because every
  deep-internal channel measured so far is unresolved-at-best while the
  external/decode-side seam holds the programme's only positive [STIPULATED:
  ASM-0802; the supporting facts are §0's nsk1 and f2b premises].
- **Endpoint, corrected:** cost parity with the external loop is NOT the sole
  endpoint — an internal placement can be worthwhile via accuracy, coverage, or
  latency-variance improvements even at equal cost. The family head-to-head
  (H-RULE-CD/KV vs H-VL at R-1) is decided on **Pareto frontiers over (accuracy on
  the covered slice, coverage reached, p95 latency, resource vector)**, with the
  pre-registered primary being frontier dominance, not a single-axis comparison
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0815; answers review "Compare Pareto frontiers"]. Registered
  projection: ASM-0806.

### 3.3a H-PS — NL→program synthesis (new; first-class)

The review's "promising missing design", adopted as a family rather than a
footnote because it attacks the programme's actual measured wall (the NL
boundary) directly instead of hoping rule knowledge emerges in a hidden layer
[STIPULATED: ASM-0815]:

- **Design shape:** parse natural input into a small executable DSL (the kernel's
  closed grammar, or a thin typed layer over it); execute deterministically
  (the µs engine); generate the surface answer FROM the checked result; abstain
  with calibration when parse confidence is low (selective prediction — the
  parser emits a calibrated confidence and the system's abstention policy is part
  of the measured product).
- Evidence status: cautioned — this is exactly the l3a-parse/a5-nl path, both
  measured FAIL at scope with the a1-hybrid front-end; H-PS is the *redesign* of
  that path with modern semantic-parsing/program-synthesis methods and calibrated
  abstention, which is why P3-D-PS and P3-D-NLB are both blocked by the
  P3-LR-PARSE lit-review (do not repeat the measured parser failure blindly).
- Relation to P3-D-NLB: NLB is the shared *gate instrument* (retention + S2
  fail-closed measurement per vertical); H-PS is an *architecture family* whose
  front-end, if it clears NLB, becomes the shared front-end other families use.
  They co-design; the gate stays independent of any one family's success.
- Cheapest falsifier: G2/G3 on the two existing verticals — oracle-parse ceiling
  (known: engine exact) vs synthesised-parse retention, against the pinned 0.90
  retention + S2 bars; then the D4 diagnostics with held-out compositions.

### 3.4 H-RB — retrieve-vs-bake (the store-placement fork, cross-cutting)

For any store-using family: store on disk + retrieval at inference (bytes cheap,
per-query resource cost) vs distilled/baked into weights (bytes expensive, zero
per-query retrieval). A pure size↔cost trade under KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2,
swept, not hypothesised: each Phase-2 winner is measured at both settings and the
Pareto points reported. (The a-e2 dense-I/O membership bound — an upper bound, §0
— stays quarantined until its consumption channel is measured.)

### 7.2 Named risks, each tied to current evidence

**R1 — the answer-key trap at programme scale (highest severity).** The whole
programme could "win" by encoding eval-adjacent content into stores. This is the
f2b confound (§2.2) writ large; the decontamination gate + factorial controls +
the SEALED post-freeze evaluation (§2.2a — new, because screening alone cannot
catch abstraction-level leakage or researcher adaptation) are the mitigation, and
the win-voiding rule is deliberately harsh.

**R2 — the coverage wall may bind before any architecture question does.**
Measured coverage against external benchmark material is approximately zero today
(0/1,550 define-lane census; g8 0/1,000; m0b 0.3542 on the *friendliest* corpus —
§0). Revision 2 turns this from a risk paragraph into the G1 GATE: the Δ_max
bound is computed on paper before any GPU spend, per family, and K-P3v2(1) makes
it a programme-kill condition. The coverage-growth/ingestion line
(`docs/next/coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md`) is the feeder workstream, its
costs logged into KOT-LIFE/1. This remains the single most likely way Programme-3
dies, and it is inherited, not new.

**R3 — the NL boundary is inherited by every real-input claim.** Both measured
crossings failed, one dangerously (§0). Revision 2's answer is structural AND
dependency-encoded: the parser is inside the measured system (§1.1), P3-D-NLB is
blocked by the semantic-parsing lit-review (don't rebuild the failed parser
naively), P3-E-NLB-1 gates every natural-input store leg of every family
(ASM-0814), H-PS attacks the boundary directly with calibrated abstention, and
K-P3v2(2)/(5) kill the programme if the boundary won't yield. The a5-nl lesson
generalises: a wrong-but-confident symbolic answer with provenance attached is
WORSE than a refusal, so fail-closedness is a scored dimension of every
architecture (§3.3), not a nicety.

**R4 — integration may not follow delivery (the nsk1 lesson).** For H-GNN and
H-RULE-ACT/HL especially: nsk1 showed content can be delivered into the residual
stream at echo grade while rescuing nothing, and text delivery measured
net-harmful (§0). Mitigation: every fusion design carries a causal behavioural
endpoint on held-out items, never a delivery endpoint alone; the placement
ordering (§3.3) starts where the executor's work is delivered by construction
(engine-derived constrained decoding); the four-stage decomposition (§3.2)
localises delivery-vs-use failures.

**R5 — H-DD rests on an unmeasured correspondence AND previously on incorrect
surgery assumptions.** The §3.5 reformulation corrects the assumptions (invariance
group, SAE geometry, width-vs-bytes, provenance) and interposes the P3-E-DD-0
causal gate; nothing yet links kernel concept vectors to any model's internal
subspaces, and the target sits far beyond measured coverage. The risk is bounded
by the gate's cheapness; the distinctive claim dies cleanly if ablation is
non-selective, reinjection non-restorative, or (i) ≈ (iii)/(iv) — registered
resolution path on ASM-0804 as amended by ASM-0816.

**R6 — the index is gameable by suite choice; the frontier by comparator choice.**
Mitigations: suite + domains pinned in P3-D-INDEX before any architecture result
exists; the threat model (§2.0) enumerates the gaming channels; the
frontier-builder pins comparators at freeze; P3-E-CAL validates the instrument on
known-ordered models; the sealed suite (§2.2a) catches adaptation to the frozen
suite.

**R7 — small-scale findings may not transfer up the ladder.** R-0/R-1 kills could
discard architectures that only work at scale. Accepted deliberately as the price
of cheap-first; hedges: the H-GU ≥3-point direction protocol (a shrinking-negative
is killed, a growing-from-negative is promoted), the same-gate-two-rungs kill rule
(not one), the G5 two-size confirmation, and P3-LR-EVAL's proxy-rung-validity
question feeding margin design. Any claim about behaviour at unrun rungs is an
extrapolation and stays out of premises.

item.text: Two central accounting problems are now clear: equal `pass@32` is being treated as equal compute, and “same model” ablations are being called compute-matched even when retrieval/search/executor costs differ. That conflicts with Programme-3’s own resource-vector standard and materially inflates the review’s decision confidence.

    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 0. Executive verdict (one paragraph)
    47	
    48	The strongest, most-replicated finding in this scope is that a **sound deterministic checker
    49	sitting in a training loop** — filtering self-generated proofs (expert iteration), providing
    50	RL reward, or supplying error messages for self-correction — reliably improves a neural
    51	proposer, with one genuinely **compute-matched** demonstration [LIT: polu-curriculum-2022:
    52	expert iteration beats proof-search-only at equal compute] and a 2025–26 trend of
    53	**recipe-over-parameters** results (8B and even 4B provers beating a 671B prover at the same
    54	pass@32 budget [LIT: goedel-v2-2025, pythagoras-2026]). The flagship small-scale existence
    55	proof is AlphaGeometry: a 151M LM trained purely on engine-generated synthetic proof traces,
    56	proposing constructions to a deterministic symbolic engine, reaching near-gold IMO geometry
    57	[LIT: alphageometry-2024] — though its search budget (beam 512 × 10,000 CPU workers) and
    58	AlphaProof's ~180k TPU-days [LIT: alphaproof-2025] mark this family's headline results as
    59	**maximally unmatched-compute**; the matched-accounting core that survives skeptical reading
    60	is much smaller (§1.3). Differentiable logic has **no matched-baseline win beyond toy KBs and
    61	module scale** and its own lineage abandoned in-loss inference for external solvers (§2). NAR
    62	shows that hard **discrete interfaces buy OOD transfer on covered algorithmic tasks**
    63	[LIT: discrete-nar-2025] while trace-trained plain transformers shortcut and fail OOD
    64	[LIT: paradox-2022, prontoqa-2023, anil-lengthgen-2022] — jointly, the literature's cleanest
    65	argument FOR the programme's bet shape (deterministic engine owns execution/search; small LM
    66	owns proposal/surface) and AGAINST hoping distilled traces alone induce a reasoner.
    67	
    68	## 1. Neural theorem proving: proposer + sound checker
    69	
    70	### 1.1 State of the art, and what exists at 100M–2B
    71	
    72	The field's architecture has been stable since 2020: a generative LM proposes proof steps
    73	(or whole proofs), a **sound proof checker** (Metamath/Lean/Isabelle kernel) validates, and
    74	a search procedure allocates samples [LIT: gptf-2020, htps-2022]. The standard yardstick is
    75	miniF2F (488 olympiad/coursework statements, cross-system) [LIT: minif2f-2022]. Frontier
    76	solve rates are now very high — miniF2F-test 84.6% pass@32 at 8B, 88–93% at 32B
    77	[LIT: goedel-v2-2025, pythagoras-2026], with the 671B comparison target carried from those
    78	papers [LIT: dsprover-v2-2025, UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS] — but **no current frontier prover
    79	is ≤2B**. What exists in/near our band:
    80	
    81	- **ReProver (299M ByT5 + premise retrieval)** — the ≤2B anchor. LeanDojo Benchmark Pass@1
    82	  51.2% (random split) / 26.3% (novel-premises split); miniF2F 26.5%; trained in ~1 GPU-week
    83	  on one A100 vs "thousands of GPU-days" for prior systems [LIT: leandojo-2023] [MEASURED at
    84	  its scope]. Two accounting reads: vs its own no-retrieval twin (47.6%/23.2%) the retrieval
    85	  win is **matched** (same model, same budget); vs GPT-4 zero-shot (29.0%/7.4%) it is
    86	  finetuned-vs-few-shot, i.e., the distill-sbs caveat applies (§4.3).
    87	- **GPT-f (160M–1.5B)** — best 56.22% held-out Metamath at **700M**; larger was not always
    88	  better at fixed data/search [LIT: gptf-2020]. Expert-iteration sampling cost ~20k V100
    89	  GPU-hours per iteration — the checker is sound but the LOOP is expensive.
    90	- **Graph2Tac (GNN-scale, Coq)** — online learning of new definitions; ≥1.48× more theorems
    91	  than CoqHammer/Proverbot9001/a transformer baseline; with a k-NN co-solver, 1.5× over its
    92	  own offline twin [LIT: graph2tac-2024]. Evidence that tiny non-transformer provers with
    93	  **definition-online-learning** beat bigger offline ones in interactive proving; matching
    94	  of the cross-system comparisons is not stated.
    95	- **AlphaGeometry's LM is 151M** (excl. embeddings), trained from scratch on 100M
    96	  engine-generated synthetic theorem+proof traces [LIT: alphageometry-2024] — inside our
    97	  band on parameters, far outside on search (§1.3).
    98	
    99	**Trend line (load-bearing for sizing):** parameters are not the binding axis in this
   100	domain. Goedel-Prover-V2-8B beats DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B at the same pass@32 metric
   101	(80× smaller) [LIT: goedel-v2-2025]; Pythagoras-Prover-4B does the same at ~167× smaller
   102	[LIT: pythagoras-2026]; both attribute the win to verifier-coupled data recipes (scaffolded
   103	/ curriculum synthesis, compiler-feedback self-correction), not architecture. [EXTRAPOLATION:
   104	that this trend continues below 2B is NOT established — no frontier-recipe prover has been
   105	published at ≤2B; this is an explicit Phase-1 open question, §7-Q1.]
   106	
   107	### 1.2 What beat matched baselines, under what accounting
   108	
   109	Skeptically filtered, the **matched** wins are:
   110	
   111	1. **Expert iteration vs proof-search-only at equal compute budget** [LIT:
   112	   polu-curriculum-2022] — the single cleanest result in the field for our purposes:
   113	   putting the sound checker's verdicts back into TRAINING beats spending the same compute
   114	   on more search. It also self-organizes a difficulty curriculum from unordered statements.
   115	2. **Retrieval vs no-retrieval, same 299M model** [LIT: leandojo-2023] — +3.6/+3.1 points;
   116	   modest but honest.
   117	3. **Proof-artifact co-training vs same model without it**, 32%→48% on held-out Lean
   118	   theorems [LIT: pact-2021] — auxiliary self-supervised tasks harvested from checker-level
   119	   artifacts are cheap signal.
   120	4. **Hammer+LM division of labour vs LM-only**, PISA 39%→57%, +8.2% solved by neither
   121	   component alone [LIT: thor-2022] — complementarity of neural proposal and deterministic
   122	   automation is additive, not just substitutive.
   123	5. **Verifier-guided self-correction** (revise on Lean compiler messages) as an ablatable
   124	   component: +2.3 points at 32B (88.1→90.4 pass@32) [LIT: goedel-v2-2025].
   125	
   126	The **unmatched** headline results, named as such: HTPS's 82.6% Metamath (vs GPT-f 56.5%)
   127	rests on online training plus large search, with no compute-matching stated [LIT: htps-2022];
   128	Kimina's 80.7% is at pass@8192 — a 256× larger sample budget than the pass@32 rows it is
   129	often tabled against [LIT: kimina-prover-2025, UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS]; AlphaProof's IMO
   130	silver used a 3B model but ~80,000 TPU-days of RL plus ~100,000 TPU-days of
   131	auto-formalization, and Test-Time RL trains on variants of the single target problem
   132	[LIT: alphaproof-2025] — an existence proof, not an economics proof.
   133	
   134	**Benchmark-quality flag:** >half of original miniF2F statements are misaligned with their
   135	informal versions; the corrected v2 drops end-to-end accuracy to 70% for the best system
   136	tested there [LIT: minif2f-v2-2025]. All miniF2F deltas above carry this caveat.
   137	
   138	### 1.3 Accounting lessons this domain teaches (adopt into every Phase-1 prereg)
   139	
   140	- **The sample budget IS the compute axis**: pass@k comparisons are meaningless across k
   141	  (32 vs 3200 vs 8192 all appear in SOTA tables) [LIT: goedel-v2-2025, stp-2025,
   142	  kimina-prover-2025]. KOT-COST/2 must pin k and wall-clock per family arm. [STIPULATED]
   143	- **Sound-checker loops fail by PLATEAU, learned-verifier loops fail by DECAY.** With a
   144	  sound checker, false positives are impossible, so self-training data stays clean; the
   145	  loop instead plateaus when provable statements run out — the binding constraint is
   146	  **statement supply**, fixed by conjecturer self-play (LeanWorkbook 13.2%→28.5%
   147	  [LIT: stp-2025]) or scaffolded synthesis [LIT: goedel-v2-2025]. With a learned verifier,
   148	  search DEGRADES below repeated sampling at large budgets because imperfect rankers prune
   149	  all valid paths [LIT: scaling-flaws-2025; owned by RAG.md §4]. Our engine is sound on its
   150	  covered slice, so the KOT analog of "verifier failure" is **coverage exhaustion**, and the
   151	  KOT analog of "statement supply" is the synthetic generator + world-layer growth rate.
   152	  [EXTRAPOLATION — this mapping is an argument, not a measurement.]
   153	- **Checker latency was never the binding constraint.** Lean/Metamath checks cost
   154	  ~10ms–seconds and every system above still profits from them at massive sample counts;
   155	  all published cost ceilings live in the neural proposer and the search. [MEASURED across
   156	  cited systems' setups; see §6 for what a µs engine changes and does not change.]
   157	
   158	## 2. Differentiable logic: the matched-baseline record is negative at scale
   159	
   160	Extending the parsing report's one-liner into the Phase-0 accounting question:
   161	
   162	- **NTP (differentiable backward chaining)** beat ComplEx on 3 of 4 KBs — all toy-sized
   163	  (Countries/Nations/Kinship/UMLS) [LIT: ntp-2017]. Its own successor line concedes the
   164	  computation graph does not scale: GNTP recovers tractability (orders-of-magnitude cheaper)
   165	  but reports "on par with NTPs" and only "competitive" link prediction on large KBs — i.e.,
   166	  **scaling recovered feasibility, not superiority over matched dedicated predictors**
   167	  [LIT: gntp-2020].

   201	## 3. Neural algorithmic reasoning: interfaces buy OOD; traces alone do not
   202	
   203	- **The benchmark and its accounting.** CLRS-30 poses 30 textbook algorithms as graph tasks
   204	  with intermediate-state "hints" and OOD-larger-input evaluation [LIT: clrs-2022]. The
   205	  headline progress — a single generalist GNN processor improving average single-task
   206	  performance >20% over prior art [LIT: generalist-nar-2022] — is **within-framework**
   207	  (GNN vs GNN, synthetic tasks, micro-F1); there is no matched external baseline in that
   208	  number. Field self-criticism: transfer to real tasks, the blueprint's stated motivation
   209	  [LIT: nar-position-2021], remains under-explored per 2025 surveys of the area [search,
   210	  2026-07-11; not load-bearing].
   211	- **The result KOT should care about: Discrete NAR.** Forcing execution trajectories into
   212	  finite predefined discrete states (separating discrete from continuous flow) yields
   213	  **perfect in- and out-of-distribution test scores** on covered tasks, single- and
   214	  multi-task, with correctness provable on arbitrary test data (ICML 2025)
   215	  [LIT: discrete-nar-2025]. This is the strongest published support for the programme's central
   216	  architectural intuition — a hard symbolic interface present during training buys OOD
   217	  transfer — with its honest scope: state supervision on closed algorithmic tasks. It is
   218	  the closest prior art to H-RULE-HL's "pinned bottleneck" and to H-GU's "symbolic
   219	  interface from step 0", and its state-supervision requirement maps to our engine's
   220	  ability to emit exact intermediate states cheaply.
   221	- **The negative space around plain transformers on traces** (jointly load-bearing for
   222	  H-GU): naive finetuning fails length generalization regardless of scale [LIT:
   223	  anil-lengthgen-2022]; with ideal position encodings + formats, 2.5× extrapolation is
   224	  achievable **but fragile across seeds and data order** [LIT: zhou-lengthgen-2024] — so
   225	  any H-GU sweep read must be powered for seed variance, or direction reads are noise
   226	  [STIPULATED → P3-D-GU prereg requirement]; LMs fine-tuned as generalist executors on
   227	  CLRS-Text confirm weak length-OOD [LIT: clrs-text-2024]; and trace-trained transformers
   228	  on compositional tasks reduce to "linearized subgraph matching" — pattern reuse, not
   229	  algorithm induction — failing OOD even when trained on explicit step-by-step traces
   230	  [LIT: faith-fate-2023] (detail in §4.2).
   231	- **Fusion prior art (hand-off to P3-LR-FUSE, not deepened here):** TransNAR — LM tokens
   232	  cross-attending to a pretrained NAR's node embeddings beats Transformer-only on
   233	  CLRS-Text in- and out-of-distribution [LIT: transnar-2024]; parameter/compute-matching
   234	  of that baseline is not stated in the abstract and the "20% absolute" folk figure is
   235	  UNVERIFIED at that precision; it also requires the graph input at inference. Logged as
   236	  a §3.2-sweep prior-art datapoint for P3-D-GNN.

   238	## 4. Proof-trace distillation into small LMs (the H-GU feed)
   239	
   240	### 4.1 What bounded-depth deduction distillation achieves
   241	
   242	Transformers CAN learn closed-world deduction over explicitly stated rules: 99%
   243	in-distribution, 95%+ at depths beyond training, with zero-shot transfer to paraphrased
   244	rulebases, at ~RoBERTa scale [LIT: ruletaker-2020]. Generating the proof matters:
   245	**iterative single-step implication generation** transfers to unseen depths and OOD
   246	theories where whole-proof generation is weaker (+9pt over prior work) [LIT:
   247	proofwriter-2021]. LoGiPT is the closest published shape to "distill kernel proof traces":
   248	fine-tune on the *revealed internal step trace of a deductive solver* so the LM emulates
   249	the solver directly — beating solver-augmented pipelines (whose NL→symbolic parse errors
   250	are fatal) and few-shot GPT-4 on ProofWriter/PrOntoQA-class tasks [LIT: logipt-2023].
   251	
   252	### 4.2 The shortcut kills (mandatory controls, not vibes)
   253	
   254	- BERT trained on SimpleLogic: near-perfect in-distribution, **fails on other distributions
   255	  of the SAME problem space** — it learned statistical features of reasoning data, not
   256	  reasoning [LIT: paradox-2022]. ⇒ Every H-GU eval MUST include within-problem-space
   257	  distribution shifts (rule-priors, branching factors), not only depth extrapolation.
   258	- CoT parsed into formal proofs shows the deficit is localized: individual deduction steps
   259	  are fine; **proof planning/search fails** when multiple valid steps exist [LIT:
   260	  prontoqa-2023]. ⇒ The engine/search harness should own planning; distillation should
   261	  target step proposal + verbalization, not search policy. [STIPULATED design prior]
   262	- Trace-trained transformers on compositional tasks (multi-digit multiplication, logic
   263	  grid puzzles, DP) collapse to linearized subgraph matching and fail OOD **even with
   264	  explicit reasoning-trace supervision** [LIT: faith-fate-2023].
   265	
   266	### 4.3 Distillation accounting (the skeptical ledger)
   267	
   268	- "770M beats 540B" [LIT: distill-sbs-2023] is **task-specific finetune vs few-shot
   269	  generalist** — NOT matched; it licenses "small specialized ≥ large generalist per task"
   270	  and nothing stronger. This is also the honest frame for ReProver-vs-GPT-4 (§1.1) and
   271	  LoGiPT-vs-GPT-4 (§4.1).
   272	- CoT distillation DOES reach our band: 125M–1.3B students gain on commonsense suites, and
   273	  **teacher sampling density is the operative variable** (multiple chains per instance)
   274	  [LIT: scotd-2023].
   275	- Verifier-filtered bootstrapping needs no human rationales: STaR's
   276	  keep-only-correct-answer loop lets 6B match a 30× larger finetuned model on
   277	  CommonsenseQA [LIT: star-2022] — with a WEAK verifier (answer string match). A sound
   278	  engine strictly strengthens this filter on the covered slice. [EXTRAPOLATION]
   279	- Synthetic-curriculum existence proofs at our scale: phi-1 (1.3B, 7B curated/synthetic
   280	  tokens, HumanEval 50.6%) with thin contamination controls — recipe inspiration, not
   281	  evidence [LIT: phi1-2023]; TinyGSM (1.3B + 1.3B learned verifier → 81.5% GSM8K, verifier
   282	  carries a large share) [LIT: tinygsm-2023; owned as analogy by PARSE.md].
   283	- The programme's own asset alignment [STIPULATED]: AlphaGeometry's decisive ingredient was
   284	  an **engine-generated synthetic corpus with exact traces** (100M theorems; ~10M with
   285	  auxiliary constructions) [LIT: alphageometry-2024]. KOT already possesses the analog: a
   286	  deterministic, seedable synthetic generator + µs engine that can emit unbounded exact
   287	  proof traces over the covered grammar with controlled depth/polarity/paraphrase axes
   288	  [MEASURED: encoder/ + poc/ infrastructure exists; coverage ceiling per m0b 0.3542
   289	  friendliest-corpus remains the binding scope limit, feasibility-synthesis §0].
   290	
   291	## 5. Executor-in-the-loop training precedents (verifier-guided training)
   292	
   293	Three rungs, by increasing integration depth — the evidence gets thinner as integration
   294	gets deeper [STIPULATED framing]:
   295	
   296	1. **Executor as data filter (expert iteration / rejection finetuning).** The
   297	   best-evidenced rung: GPT-f value-function training from its own verified searches
   298	   [LIT: gptf-2020]; compute-matched superiority over search-only [LIT:
   299	   polu-curriculum-2022]; HTPS online training [LIT: htps-2022]; ReST-EM's binary-feedback
   300	   EM loop (gains scale with size, diminish over iterations) [LIT: restem-2024]; STaR at
   301	   6B [LIT: star-2022]; plateau caused by statement supply, fixed by self-play
   302	   conjecturing [LIT: stp-2025].
   303	2. **Executor as RL reward / feedback channel.** RLPAF (Lean pass/fail as reward)
   304	   [LIT: dsprover15-2024]; CodeRL's unit-test critic at 770M-class scale [LIT:
   305	   coderl-2022]; RLEF — training the model to CONSUME execution feedback across turns cuts
   306	   inference samples by an order of magnitude at 8B/70B [LIT: rlef-2024]; Goedel-V2's
   307	   compiler-feedback self-correction as an ablatable +2.3pt component [LIT: goedel-v2-2025];
   308	   Logic-LM's solver-error self-refinement at inference [LIT: logic-lm-2023]. Directly
   309	   licenses training the H-VL retry policy rather than only bolting retry on.
   310	3. **Executor literally in the loss (differentiable).** Semantic loss (circuit-compiled
   311	   constraints) [LIT: semantic-loss-2018]; blackbox-differentiation through exact solvers
   312	   (Gurobi/Blossom V/Dijkstra as layers with informative gradients) [LIT:
   313	   blackbox-solvers-2020]; DeepProbLog/Scallop (§2). All module-scale; **no published
   314	   demonstration at ≥100M-LM scale with a logic engine in the loss**. This rung is
   315	   unprecedented, not disproven. [MEASURED absence, at review scope]
   316	
   317	**The scaling-flaws boundary** (re-anchored from RAG.md §4): the decay of verifier-guided
   318	search at large budgets is a LEARNED-verifier pathology (misranking prunes valid paths)
   319	[LIT: scaling-flaws-2025]. None of the sound-checker systems above exhibit it; they
   320	plateau on statement supply instead [LIT: stp-2025]. For KOT this cleanly separates the
   321	two failure surfaces Phase-1 must instrument: engine **coverage** (the sound analog of
   322	verifier failure) and generator **supply/diversity** (the analog of conjecturer collapse).
   323	
   324	## 6. Transfer to a µs deterministic executor (the verifier-loop bet's prior art)
   325	
   326	What the literature establishes, and exactly where KOT's premise goes beyond it:
   327	
   328	- **Small proposer + deterministic engine beating larger neural-alone: established at
   329	  system level.** AlphaGeometry (151M LM + DD/AR engine, 25/30 vs prior best 10; the
   330	  oft-quoted GPT-4 0/30 comparison was not confirmed in my Nature fetch — UNVERIFIED)
   331	  [LIT: alphageometry-2024], with the successor showing the neural side kept scaling
   332	  without the symbolic engine capping the system (84% of 25 years of IMO geometry)
   333	  [LIT: alphageometry2-2025]; ReProver-299M's premise-retrieval system over GPT-4 zero-shot
   334	  (unmatched accounting noted) [LIT: leandojo-2023]; Thor's +18pt from adding hammers
   335	  [LIT: thor-2022]. In every case the engine contributes soundness and/or exhaustive local
   336	  deduction; the LM contributes exactly what the engine cannot enumerate (constructions,
   337	  premise relevance, tactic priors).
   338	- **Checker speed has never been the tested variable.** Published checkers cost ~10ms–s per
   339	  call; systems compensate with parallelism (10,000 CPU workers [LIT: alphageometry-2024])
   340	  and still win. A 5.29–7.82 µs/query engine [MEASURED: programme premise,
   341	  programme-3 §2 PREMISE block] is ~10³–10⁵× cheaper per check than any executor in this
   342	  literature. Two consequences [EXTRAPOLATION, both]:
   343	  (a) every verified-training-loop precedent in §5 becomes cheaper in its checking leg —
   344	  but since checking was never the cost ceiling (§1.3), **the µs property does NOT by
   345	  itself improve the loop's economics**; proposer sampling still dominates. The honest
   346	  claim is "removes a cost term", not "changes the regime", for training.
   347	  (b) the genuinely novel regime is **per-token/inference-time integration**: at µs per
   348	  check, the engine can be consulted inside the decode loop (H-RULE-CD's engine-derived
   349	  continuation sets; H-VL's verify-retry with negligible verify overhead) at a cost no
   350	  published executor could sustain. The literature has NO precedent for a
   351	  semantic executor consulted per decoding step — the nearest cousins are grammar-mask
   352	  automata (syntax-only; owned by the parsing report) and KV-side memories (no execution).
   353	  This is where the µs property is a real differentiator, and it is an H-RULE/H-VL design
   354	  question, not an H-GU one. [STIPULATED hand-off]
   355	- **The engine as data factory is the underexploited transfer.** AlphaGeometry's synthetic
   355	- **The engine as data factory is the underexploited transfer.** AlphaGeometry's synthetic
   356	  corpus and Goedel-V2's scaffolded synthesis both use the deterministic side to
   357	  MANUFACTURE the training distribution [LIT: alphageometry-2024, goedel-v2-2025]. A µs
   358	  engine makes trace generation effectively free at any curriculum size — the one place
   359	  its speed directly buys training-side value. [EXTRAPOLATION]
   360	- **What nothing in this literature licenses:** that any of this closes the NL boundary
   361	  (all cited systems consume FORMAL inputs or curated problem statements — the l3a-parse /
   362	  a5-nl FAILs stand untouched [MEASURED: registry/verdicts, via feasibility-synthesis §0]);
   363	  and that verifier-loop gains are kernel-CONTENT-specific rather than
   364	  correct-alignment-specific (the f2b confound; unresolved pending knull / f2b-transfer
   365	  [MEASURED: registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license]).

   680	### 3.3 H-RULE-{CD, KV, AD, ACT, HL} — rules-based inference built into the LLM: the placement analysis

    46	- CPU-seconds;

    51	- throughput under a fixed concurrency distribution.

MLPerf uses explicit latency, throughput, compliance, and full-system power methodology for exactly these reasons; the programme should borrow that measurement discipline rather than relying on query-level `nvidia-smi` readings alone. item.text:
## Verdict

Do not commit this as the final P3-LR-NTP deliverable yet. It is a strong draft with a largely real bibliography and several useful design controls, but its central accounting and uncertainty claims are too confident for a decision-grade Phase-0 review.

I found no obviously fabricated paper among the 53 entries. Most spot-checked titles, identifiers, and headline numbers are real. The problem is characterization: `verified:true` often means “a primary page was fetched,” not that the detailed claim, ablation, or resource comparison was independently verified. [NTP.sources.jsonl](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.sources.jsonl) therefore overstates its evidence status.

## Three most important concerns, ranked

1. **“Matched” and “same budget” are used incorrectly.**

   The executive verdict treats equal `pass@32` as equal compute ([NTP.md:48](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:48)). It is not. Equal sample count does not equal equal tokens, active parameters, accelerator work, latency, search overhead, or energy. Goedel and Pythagoras really do report 8B/4B beating DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B at `pass@32`, but this establishes parameter efficiency under a common metric—not matched total compute. [Goedel-Prover-V2](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.03613), [Pythagoras-Prover](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12594).

   This is especially misleading because the 671B comparator is an MoE-derived system: quoting total parameters as an 80×/167× inference advantage is not an active-compute comparison.

   Likewise, ReProver retrieval, PACT co-training, Thor’s hammer, and Goedel self-correction are controlled ablations, but not demonstrated resource-matched wins merely because the base model or `k` is shared ([NTP.md:109](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:109)). Retrieval, extra training objectives, compiler retries, and ATP calls add costs. Only the Polu expert-iteration result explicitly claims same-compute superiority in its abstract. [Formal Mathematics Statement Curriculum Learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.01344).

   This directly conflicts with the GPT-5.6 review’s resource-vector requirement and Programme-3’s own rule that no scalar/FLOP diagnostic alone licenses “compute-matched” ([programme-3:337](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:337)).

2. **The review turns scoped evidence into overly broad field laws and closures.**

   The dichotomy “sound-checker loops fail by plateau; learned-verifier loops fail by decay” ([NTP.md:143](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:143)) is memorable but too categorical. STP identifies scarcity of correct proofs on a fixed statement supply as one plateau mechanism; it does not establish statement supply as the universal binding constraint. Exploration quality, model capacity, proof representation, search policy, formal-library coverage, and training stability can also bind.

   “False positives are impossible” is only true for validity relative to a checker’s formal statement, axioms, implementation, and trusted kernel. It does not protect against wrong formalization—the programme’s main measured NL failure.

   Similarly, differentiable logic is reasonably low-priority, but this review has not established a field-wide negative matched-baseline record sufficient to “close” the architecture. CTP reports SOTA on CLUTRR, not a resource-matched experiment; Logic-LM is not evidence that the differentiable-logic lineage “abandoned” in-loss reasoning. These are separate, coexisting research lines. The defensible decision is “do not prioritize in Phase 1 absent stronger evidence,” not “uncertainty closed.”

3. **The hand-off assumes KOT-specific transfer that the literature does not establish.**

   The review usefully preserves the NL/coverage caveat, but still moves too quickly from Lean/Metamath/geometry systems to KOT design specifics. KOT’s µs query executor has not yet been shown to expose the proof states, legal-action sets, compiler-style diagnostics, or search transitions required by the cited training recipes. Cheap checking is not automatically cheap trace generation, conjecture generation, proof search, tokenization, storage, or training.

   In particular, declaring H-RULE-CD’s “novelty claim safe” ([NTP.md:430](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:430)) is premature while P3-LR-RULE—the review explicitly tasked with executor-backed constrained decoding—is still pending. Execution-guided partial-program decoding is already acknowledged elsewhere. This review can say “no precedent found in this search,” not “no precedent exists.”

## Factual and citation errors

- **miniF2F-v2 direction is wrong.** The review says the corrected benchmark “drops end-to-end accuracy to 70%” ([NTP.md:134](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:134)). The paper reports roughly 36–40% on the original end-to-end pipeline and an improvement to 70% after correction. The correction exposes severe original misalignment, but 70% is an increase, not a drop. [miniF2F-Lean Revisited](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.03108).

- **`pass@32 budget` is mislabeled as compute.** It is a shared sample count/metric only. The document itself later says wall-clock must also be pinned, making the executive statement internally inconsistent.

- **“Matched” overlabels four of the five listed wins.** ReProver, PACT, Thor, and Goedel are useful component ablations; the cited pages do not establish equality on Programme-3’s accelerator/CPU/I/O/energy/latency resource vector.

- **The 671B ratios need an MoE qualification.** Total-parameter ratios are real as paper-reported marketing comparisons, but they should not be presented as compute ratios or evidence that parameters are non-binding.

- **“Checker latency was never the binding constraint” is unverified.** No cited cross-system latency study establishes the asserted “10 ms–seconds” range or that every published cost ceiling lies only in proposal/search ([NTP.md:153](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/lit/NTP.md:153)). Label this a hypothesis/inference, not `[MEASURED across cited systems]`.

- **“A µs engine makes trace generation effectively free at any curriculum size” is false as written.** Engine execution may be negligible, but generator sampling, search, trace serialization, deduplication, storage, training-token processing, and validation still scale.

- **“Sound engine strictly strengthens” STaR’s answer filter is not a strict dominance result.** It improves formal validity precision on covered statements while sacrificing coverage and remaining vulnerable to specification/formalization error.

- **The AlphaGeometry and AlphaProof figures checked out.** AlphaGeometry used beam 512, four GPU workers and a shared pool of 10,000 CPU solver workers; AlphaProof reports about 80,000 TPU-days for RL plus 100,000 for auto-formalization. [AlphaGeometry](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06747-5), [AlphaProof](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-09833-y).

- **Discrete NAR is accurately summarized at abstract level**, including perfect reported test scores and conditional correctness proofs, but its closed-task/state-supervision scope must remain prominent. [Discrete NAR](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v267/rodionov25a.html).

## Phase-1 de-risking assessment

The review does add valuable controls:

- within-problem distribution shifts, depth extrapolation, and multiple seeds;
- explicit separation of formal-input results from the NL boundary;
- `pass@k` pinning;
- staged verifier-loop variants;
- statement-supply measurement;
- emulation-versus-execution soundness accounting.

But it does not yet close the key Phase-1 uncertainties. The design beads should additionally require:

- an oracle/API gate proving that KOT can emit the states, actions, traces, diagnostics, and continuation sets each proposed arm needs;
- full resource-vector accounting for every ablation, not merely equal model size or `pass@k`;
- factorial separation of synthetic curriculum, proof traces, checker filtering, retry feedback, and hard state interfaces;
- formalization/specification-error accounting distinct from checker validity;
- “no precedent found” language until P3-LR-RULE and P3-LR-PARSE complete their adjacent searches.

## Recommendation

Return for a narrow revision, not a rewrite. Correct the miniF2F-v2 error, downgrade the “matched” ledger, replace equal-`pass@32` compute language, qualify the MoE comparison, remove universal latency/plateau/free-generation claims, and change architecture “closures” and novelty claims into scoped Phase-1 prioritization decisions.

After those changes, it would be sound to commit as P3-LR-NTP. In its present form, it is not sufficiently reliable as the final decision record.
